mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 8.5% triggers a labor market shock that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 8.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 90 triggers a supply-side shock that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 90 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1.2 million triggers a supply-side shock that increases corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP below 13000 triggers a demand-side shock that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 13000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in initial unemployment claims above 300K triggers a labor market shock that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 300K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real disposable personal income above 130000 triggers a demand-side shock that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 130000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
